aliases: Spiced grilled chicken with steamed rice

english: Spiced grilled chicken with steamed rice

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for ayam panggang with rice; mapped to the nearest parent class 'chicken dish' (FOODON_03315595) as a defensible approximate anchor.
ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken, grilled — used as the closest FoodEx2 anchor for the grilled chicken component of this composite dish.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a composite code for grilled chicken with rice as a single dish; A0CKP (grilled chicken) is used as the primary protein anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical food concept identified for this composite Malay dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 350 g, comprising roughly 150 g of grilled chicken and 200 g of steamed white rice, as typically plated at Singaporean hawker centres and Malay food stalls.

nutrient_source.title: Ayam Panggang with Rice — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Ayam Panggang with Rice (cr_id F05100002), confidence 0.95. All nine reported nutrients are used as-is.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; and University of Sydney GI Database entry for white rice (steamed, Asian-style)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the composite dish ayam panggang with rice. GI of 65 is estimated from the dominant carbohydrate component: steamed white rice (jasmine-type), which has a well-established GI of approximately 64–72 in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international tables. The grilled chicken component contributes negligible carbohydrate and does not materially alter the composite GI. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 34.5) / 100 ≈ 22.4, using 350 g serving with 15.47 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 319 mg per 100 g — primarily from soy sauce and kecap manis in the marinade
hypertension.guidance: A 350 g serving delivers approximately 1,117 mg sodium (nearly half the WHO daily limit of 2,000 mg); individuals with hypertension should request reduced-sodium marinade, avoid adding extra soy sauce or sambal at the table, and limit portion size to 200–250 g.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycaemic load (GL ≈ 22 per standard serving) driven by white rice with a low dietary fibre content of 0.89 g per 100 g
type2_diabetes.guidance: The white rice component produces a high glycaemic load per serving; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should substitute white rice with brown rice or cauliflower rice, reduce the rice portion to 100–120 g, and pair with additional non-starchy vegetables to blunt postprandial glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium content (319 mg/100 g) and moderate saturated fat (~1.8 g/100 g); grilled preparation avoids excess dietary fat from frying
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The grilled (not fried) preparation keeps total fat at 6.5 g per 100 g and saturated fat low (~1.8 g/100 g), which is favourable; however, the sodium load from the marinade warrants portion control and reduced-sodium preparation for individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Grilled preparation avoids AGE-generating deep-frying; turmeric and lemongrass in marinade provide polyphenol-based anti-inflammatory benefit
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis who are sensitive to nightshades should request chilli-free preparation, and should avoid charred portions of the chicken where advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) may concentrate.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ayam Panggang with Rice is a popular Malay-Singaporean dish consisting of grilled chicken marinated in a spiced blend typically including turmeric, lemongrass, chilli, garlic, ginger, soy sauce, and kecap manis (sweet soy sauce), served alongside steamed white rice. The chicken is grilled over charcoal or a gas grill, producing a lightly charred, aromatic exterior with a moist, tender interior. The flavour profile is savoury, mildly spicy, and subtly sweet from the kecap manis glaze. Per 100 g, the dish provides 188 kcal, 16.9 g protein, 6.5 g fat, and 15.5 g carbohydrate, with sodium at 319 mg and dietary fibre at only 0.89 g. The high protein content from chicken supports satiety and muscle maintenance. The low fibre and high-GI white rice component result in a high glycaemic load per standard serving (GL ≈ 22), making portion control important for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. Sodium from the marinade is a consideration for hypertension management. The dish is halal-certified in most hawker and restaurant contexts in Singapore and Malaysia. It is commonly consumed as a lunch or dinner main course at hawker centres, Malay food stalls, and casual restaurants across Singapore and Malaysia.
common_user_queries: Calories in ayam panggang with rice
Is grilled chicken with rice good for diabetics?
How much protein in ayam panggang with rice?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05100002) with a reported confidence of 0.95. This is the primary approved source for Singaporean dishes. GI is estimated from the dominant carbohydrate component (steamed white rice) using the University of Sydney GI Database, which is the approved GI source.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite Malay dish is not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry (F05100002) as of 2026-04-16. cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g set to -1: these micronutrients are not reported in the HPB Foodo source entry for this dish. potassium_mg value of 230 mg is an estimate based on the approximate potassium contribution of grilled chicken (~250 mg/100 g per USDA SR Legacy) and steamed white rice (~35 mg/100 g per USDA SR Legacy) in the proportions typical of this dish; this value should be confirmed against a direct HPB or ICMR-NIN measurement when available. saturated_fat_g of 1.8 g is estimated from the fat composition of grilled chicken thigh/drumstick (approximately 27–28% of total fat as saturated) consistent with USDA FoodData Central data for grilled chicken; the HPB source does not report saturated fat separately. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0 (confirmed negligible for grilled chicken and steamed rice preparation — no hydrogenated fats used). The input record incorrectly listed primary_cuisine as 'western'; corrected to 'Malay / Singaporean' based on the dish name, description, and food composition. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on steamed white rice (jasmine-type) from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for the composite dish was found in approved sources.
